The report said Trump told Netanyahu that the United States was close to reaching a favourable agreement with Tehran and asked him to remain patient. articles.title: Oil producers approve supply boost as Middle East tensions persist
articles.summary: Oil producers approve supply boost as Middle East tensions persist OPEC+ has agreed to increase oil production quotas by 188,000 barrels per day in July, with ministers from Saudi Arabia, Russia, Iraq, Kuwait, Kazakhstan, Algeria and Oman approving the move during a virtual meeting. In a statement, the group said the increase is intended to support oil market stability and allow members to accelerate compensation cuts amid elevated oil prices. However, analysts questioned the practical impact of the decision while disruptions continue to affect shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: UK urges Iran and Israel to de-escalate after renewed attacks
articles.summary: UK urges Iran and Israel to de-escalate after renewed attacks UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper has urged Iran and Israel to de-escalate after renewed attacks threatened to widen the regional war. “The resumption of conflict between Iran and Israel is in no one’s interest,” Cooper said in a post on X. “Both sides must show restraint and de-escalate immediately,” she added. Cooper said “negotiations must continue towards the lasting settlement that we all need, for peace and stability in the region, and for the full restoration of global trade”.

Iran’s Foreign Ministry said the talks focused on the “latest developments” in the region and Tehran’s response to Israel’s “repeated violations of the ceasefire in Lebanon”.
